action_confirm_delete: This item will be permanently deleted. Continue?
onboarding_intro: Clavix is an open-source desktop client for Vaultwarden and Bitwarden. It reads your existing vault — no data is sent anywhere other than to your own server.
onboarding_what_1: Read, search, and copy your logins, notes, cards, identities, and SSH keys.
onboarding_what_2: Drag and drop to organize items into your folders and collections.
onboarding_what_3: Works offline after a first sync (encrypted local cache).
onboarding_alpha_body: The cryptography has not yet been audited by a third party and the API may change. Please read the disclaimer before using Clavix with a sensitive vault.
qr_hint: Point the camera at a TOTP QR code (otpauth://). It will be read automatically.
editor_owner_locked: Can't change an existing item's owner here — use share instead.
ssh_agent_hint: Expose the SSH keys (Ed25519 and RSA) from your vault over a Unix socket, like ssh-agent. Configure it in your shell with the SSH_AUTH_SOCK variable.
ssh_agent_env_mismatch: SSH_AUTH_SOCK is set to {current}. Export the path above to use Clavix's keys.
ssh_agent_env_unset: SSH_AUTH_SOCK is not set. Export the path above in your shell so ssh uses Clavix's keys.
ssh_passphrase_required: This key is passphrase-protected — enter the passphrase to decrypt it.
ssh_passphrase_hint: The passphrase is not kept — the key will be stored decrypted inside the vault, the same way Bitwarden Desktop does.
ssh_generate_hint: Creates a fresh Ed25519 keypair on the fly. Or paste an existing key below.
yubikey_unlock_section_hint: Once enabled, the encrypted master key on disk is protected by your Yubikey instead of your master password. Touching the key unlocks the vault — the master password still works as a fallback.
yubikey_unlock_warning: Someone who steals both this computer and your Yubikey can unlock the vault — equivalent to stealing the master password. The computer alone is not enough.
yubikey_unlock_enrolled: Yubikey registered — the next unlock will offer the touch shortcut.
yubikey_unlock_stale: The master password was changed on another client — unlock with the password, then re-enrol the Yubikey.
import_hint: Pick a KeePassXC CSV export (Database → Export → CSV…) or a native .kdbx file. CSV columns are read positionally as Title / Username / Password / URL / Notes / TOTP / Group; for .kdbx you'll be prompted for the database master password.
import_kdbx_password_hint: The password is consumed once to decrypt the .kdbx file. Clavix never stores it.
import_empty: No usable rows found in the file.
import_create_folders: Create a folder for each value in the Group column
import_already_present: {count} entry/entries are already in the vault (same name and username): they will be skipped, and your edits kept.
import_notes_too_long: {count} entry/entries have very long notes. They stay selected and will be attempted: a server with the default limit (10,000 characters once encrypted, i.e. about 7,400 typed — a PGP key exceeds it) will reject them, but on Vaultwarden with INCREASE_NOTE_SIZE_LIMIT=true (limit raised to 100,000) they'll import fine.
export_warning: ⚠ The exported file contains your passwords in plaintext on disk. Store it somewhere safe and delete it as soon as you no longer need it.
export_hint: Bitwarden CSV format — imports directly into Bitwarden Desktop. Cards, identities, SSH keys and items in the trash are not exported (the CSV schema does not support them).
export_nothing: Pick at least one type to export.
audit_privacy_note: Checks your passwords against HIBP using k-anonymity: only 5 hex characters of the SHA-1 hash ever leave your machine — never the password itself.
audit_reused_count: {count} group(s) of items sharing the same password:
generator_empty_charset: Select at least one character type.
two_factor_yubikey_help: Touch your YubiKey to auto-fill the code (44 characters).
two_factor_unsupported: 2FA provider "{provider}" is not supported by Clavix yet.
two_factor_webauthn_help: Plug in your FIDO2 security key (YubiKey, SoloKey, etc.) and tap it once it blinks.
two_factor_webauthn_no_challenge: The server sent no WebAuthn challenge. Vaultwarden only generates one when its DOMAIN setting is a hostname: with an IP address, WebAuthn is disabled (the spec forbids it). Check DOMAIN on the server, and re-register the key if you change it.
folder_delete_confirm: Delete folder “{name}”? {count} item(s) will be detached (not deleted) and appear under “All items”.
folder_delete_confirm_cascade: Delete “{name}” and {subCount} sub-folder(s)? {itemCount} item(s) will be detached (not deleted) and appear under “All items”.
items_gated_body: Type a search or pick a folder on the left. The vault's {count} items stay hidden until you narrow things down.
settings_require_narrowing_on: Wait for a search or a folder
settings_yubikey_ask_pin_hint: Only use this if your Yubikey was set up WITHOUT a PIN. A key enrolled with a PIN must be unlocked with that PIN — otherwise unlocking will fail. To switch modes, re-enrol the Yubikey accordingly.
settings_hide_dock_on_tray: Hide dock icon when in tray
err_auth_two_factor_invalid: The 2FA code is invalid or expired — try again.
err_auth_refresh_expired: Your session has expired — please sign in again.
err_auth_captcha_required: Vaultwarden requires a captcha. Solve it on the web UI, then retry.
err_auth_user_not_found: No account exists for this email on this server.
err_not_authenticated: No active session — please sign in.
err_yubikey_no_device: No security key detected — plug in your Yubikey and retry.
err_yubikey_unwrap_failed: Couldn't unlock with this Yubikey. Check the PIN: a key set up with a PIN must be unlocked with that PIN (and vice versa). If the master password changed, re-enrol the Yubikey.
